segments.text: This for the ones nobody checks on
Because they look like they got it together
Bills paid, kids fed, smile ready
But somewhere between Queens and Brooklyn
Under the elevator
Somebody is asking
Is
this really addiction?
You work hard, you deserve peace
Come here
Queens Boulevard got my promises
Brooklyn nights got my shame
I keep showing
I keep showing
I keep showing up like I'm solid
I keep coming home the same
Rent paid, lights on, fridge full
Everybody thinks I'm blessed
I keep my body right, keep my money good
Still got smoke inside my chest
Corner store blowing like a chapel
Midnight glass behind the door
I tell myself I'm only looking
But my hand already knows
Long day
Don't make it difficult for me to see you
One little flame, one little drink
One little sleep
And it don't feel like addiction
It feels like I'm still in control
Like I can stop when I'm ready
Like I can leave it alone
I quit three days just to prove it
Then came back like I won
But that ain't freedom
That's just losing with a different kind of girl
The cage don't always rattle
Sometimes it sings
I'm chasing the dragon under the other lake
Trying to catch what don't come back no more
First time flu, now I'm stuck here waiting
With my heart face down on the floor
It don't feel like addiction
It feels like tomorrow
Feels like one more night
Feels like my old sorrow
I wanna quit but I want that fire
I want the truth but I love the liar
And the dragon don't fly
No more
On the J train with my jaw tight
On the seven with my head low
Every ass says buy your freedom
Every craving says don't let go
I got side dreams, side gigs
Plans folded in my notes
But I keep feeding little habits
That keep standing on my throat
I say next week like a prayer
Say last time like a song
Say I'm back to where I was
Good when I'm lying
Say I'm fine when I'm gone
Baby you're here
I'm the only thing clear
When the city gets heavy
I disappear your fear
You call me poison but I call you mine
You call it weakness, I call it time
Don't call me yours
Then why do you answer?
Addiction don't always kick the door
Sometimes it uses your own voice
Sometimes it uses your own voice
It says
you earned it
At the counter with the music low
Caught in my head, heart in my throat
I ask myself do I need this?
Then laugh like it's some kind of joke
What else could I do with this money?
What else could I do?
What else could I do with this pain?
What man keeps building the future?
Them burning the bridge in the rain
I'm chasing the dragon under the elevator
Trying to catch what don't come back
Trying to catch what don't come back
I can make it feel close, I can open that door
It don't feel like addiction,
it feels like tomorrow
Feels like one more night,
feels like moral sorrow
Borrow it baby, you can pay it later
And the dragon don't fly no more
To the one saying I can stop,
but never really stops
This is the sign, not shame, not judgment
Come back home,
do I really need this,
do I really want this
Am I really free if I keep running from the honest
Do I wanna stop, or do I wanna hide
Do I want my life back, or another alibi
Nobody knows you like I know you
Nobody holds you like I hold you
That's not love, that's not peace
That's you taking little pieces out of me
I don't need to hit bottom just to prove the bottom's real
I don't need another bottle just to tell me
How to feel, help me, hear me
I'm still in here, don't fear me
I look alright, but I'm not okay
I keep surviving, but I wanna change
Call me, hold me, don't shame me
Just know me, I need love, I need truth
I need somebody to pull me through
One more night, one more flame, one more
I'm not chasing the dragon
Under the elevator
Not tonight, not like before
First time's wrong and I'm done here waiting
For a ghost to get me
It did feel like addiction
When I finally saw the chain
It did feel like a prison
When I called it
by its name
I wanna live
More than I want that fire
I want the truth
More than I want the liar
And the dragon don't fly
No, I don't want the fire
No more
You'll miss me by morning
Maybe I will
You call me when it hurts
I'll call someone real
You'll come back to me
Not this time
For anybody fighting quiet
You are not the thing you're fighting
Cry out,
reach out
Come back
The night is loud
But you are louder
Help me, I'm ready
One more
One more
No
Give me an
One more
Give me an answer
It's just most things that aren't important to me
